title: Algorithmic fairness assessment with clinicalfair
text: clinicalfair evaluates algorithmic fairness clinical prediction models across demographic subgroups. motivated regulatory expectations transparency clinical AI evaluation.

title: Algorithmic fairness assessment with clinicalfair
code: library(clinicalfair) data(compas_sim) str(compas_sim) #> 'data.frame':    1000 obs. of  3 variables: #>  $ risk_score: num  0.486 0.268 0.168 0.189 0.675 ... #>  $ recidivism: int  1 0 0 0 1 0 1 0 1 0 ... #>  $ race      : chr  "Black" "Black" "White" "Black" ...

title: Algorithmic fairness assessment with clinicalfair
code: fd <- fairness_data(   predictions    = compas_sim$risk_score,   labels         = compas_sim$recidivism,   protected_attr = compas_sim$race ) fd #>  #> ── Fairness evaluation data #> n = 1000 | prevalence = 0.457 #> Groups: "Black, White" #> Reference: "Black" | Threshold: 0.5

title: Algorithmic fairness assessment with clinicalfair
code: fm <- fairness_metrics(fd) fm #>  #> ── Fairness metrics (reference: Black) #> # A tibble: 14 × 5 #>    group metric          value ratio difference #>  * <chr> <chr>           <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 Black selection_rate 0.571  1        0       #>  2 Black tpr            0.902  1        0       #>  3 Black fpr            0.276  1        0       #>  4 Black ppv            0.744  1        0       #>  5 Black accuracy       0.808  1        0       #>  6 Black brier          0.152  1        0       #>  7 Black auc            0.901  1        0       #>  8 White selection_rate 0.291  0.510   -0.280   #>  9 White tpr            0.573  0.635   -0.329   #> 10 White fpr            0.0724 0.262   -0.204   #> 11 White ppv            0.860  1.16     0.116   #> 12 White accuracy       0.773  0.957   -0.0346  #> 13 White brier          0.156  1.03     0.00381 #> 14 White auc            0.887  0.984   -0.0148

title: Algorithmic fairness assessment with clinicalfair
code: rpt <- fairness_report(fd) rpt #>  #> ── Fairness Report #> Reference group: "Black" #>  #> ! 3 disparity flag(s): #> White / selection_rate: ratio = 0.51 #> White / tpr: ratio = 0.635 #> White / fpr: ratio = 0.262 #>  #> 3 metric(s) violate the four-fifths rule (ratio outside [0.8, 1.25]). Consider #> threshold adjustment or model recalibration.

title: Algorithmic fairness assessment with clinicalfair
code: autoplot(fm)

title: Algorithmic fairness assessment with clinicalfair
code: plot_calibration(fd)

title: Algorithmic fairness assessment with clinicalfair
code: mit <- threshold_optimize(fd, objective = "equalized_odds") mit #>  #> ── Threshold optimization (equalized_odds) #> Black: threshold = 0.58 #> White: threshold = 0.42 #>  #> Accuracy: 0.794 -> 0.808

title: Algorithmic fairness assessment with clinicalfair
code: set.seed(42) n <- nrow(compas_sim) intersectional_fairness(   predictions = compas_sim$risk_score,   labels      = compas_sim$recidivism,   race        = compas_sim$race,   age_group   = sample(c("Young", "Old"), n, replace = TRUE) ) #>  #> ── Fairness metrics (reference: Black x Young) #> # A tibble: 28 × 5 #>    group         metric         value ratio difference #>  * <chr>         <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 Black x Old   selection_rate 0.565 0.980   -0.0117  #>  2 Black x Old   tpr            0.905 1.01     0.00685 #>  3 Black x Old   fpr            0.25  0.827   -0.0525  #>  4 Black x Old   ppv            0.770 1.07     0.0534  #>  5 Black x Old   accuracy       0.825 1.04     0.0347  #>  6 Black x Old   brier          0.141 0.870   -0.0210  #>  7 Black x Old   auc            0.922 1.05     0.0431  #>  8 Black x Young selection_rate 0.577 1        0       #>  9 Black x Young tpr            0.899 1        0       #> 10 Black x Young fpr            0.302 1        0       #> # ℹ 18 more rows

what: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
title: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
text: FDA’s 2021 Action Plan AI/ML-Based Software Medical Device made one thing clear: developers clinical prediction models expected demonstrate algorithms perform equitably across patient subgroups. agency prescribe specific fairness metrics thresholds, direction travel unmistakable — transparency differential performance across demographic groups becoming regulatory expectation, merely academic aspiration. biostatistician clinical informaticist tasked conducting audit, practical question immediate: given trained prediction model labelled evaluation cohort, systematically assess whether model’s error rates, calibration, selection behaviour differ across protected groups? disparities exist, available post-hoc remediation options preserve model interpretability? clinicalfair R package, now CRAN, designed answer questions. provides model-agnostic fairness auditing workflow takes predicted probabilities, true labels, protected attributes inputs returns group-stratified metrics, regulatory flag detection, visualisations, threshold-based mitigation — without modifying underlying model.

title: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
text: entry point fairness_data(), bundles predictions, outcomes, group membership validated container: reference group — disparity ratios computed — selected automatically group highest selection rate, following convention disparities measured relative -selected group. default can overridden. fairness_metrics() computes group-wise performance across seven metrics: metrics span major fairness criteria literature: selection rate (demographic parity), true positive rate false positive rate (equalised odds, per Hardt, Price, Srebro 2016), positive predictive value (predictive parity), AUC, accuracy, Brier score. Bootstrap confidence intervals, requested, use percentile resampling within group independently. metric reported alongside ratio absolute difference relative reference group — two quantities matter regulatory assessment.
code: library(clinicalfair) data(compas_sim)  fd <- fairness_data(   predictions = compas_sim$risk_score,   labels      = compas_sim$recidivism,   protected_attr = compas_sim$race ) fm <- fairness_metrics(fd, ci = TRUE, n_boot = 2000)

title: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
text: fairness_report() automates detection disparities exceed four-fifths rule threshold (ratio 0.8 1.25), standard borrowed employment discrimination law (EEOC Uniform Guidelines) increasingly referenced algorithmic fairness contexts: report object contains flagged metrics, narrative recommendation, full metrics table. teams preparing regulatory submissions hospital committee presentations, structured output reduces gap statistical computation actionable reporting.
code: rpt <- fairness_report(fd) rpt

title: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
text: Three visualisation functions expose different dimensions differential performance: calibration plot deserves particular attention. Obermeyer et al. (2019) demonstrated Science widely deployed healthcare algorithm exhibited severe calibration disparities across racial groups — predicted risk score, Black patients substantially sicker White patients. Differential calibration arguably clinically consequential form algorithmic unfairness, decile calibration plot makes immediately visible.
code: autoplot(fm)           # Disparity bar plots, faceted by metric plot_roc(fd)           # ROC curves stratified by group plot_calibration(fd)   # Decile calibration curves by group

title: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
text: Single-attribute fairness analysis can mask disparities emerge intersection multiple protected characteristics. model may appear fair respect race fair respect sex, yet exhibit significant disparities Black women specifically. intersectional_fairness() addresses directly: Groups minimum size threshold dropped warning, preventing unstable estimates small cells. follows methodological guidance Buolamwini Gebru (2018), whose Gender Shades study established intersectional evaluation standard algorithmic fairness.
code: isf <- intersectional_fairness(   predictions = compas_sim$risk_score,   labels      = compas_sim$recidivism,   race        = compas_sim$race,   sex         = sample(c("Male", "Female"), nrow(compas_sim), replace = TRUE),   min_group_size = 30 )

title: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
text: disparities identified, threshold_optimize() searches group-specific decision thresholds minimise disparity maintaining accuracy floor: function supports two objectives: equalised odds (minimising TPR FPR disparity) demographic parity (equalising selection rates). optimisation uses grid search candidate thresholds, accuracy constraint ensuring mitigation degrade overall model performance clinically acceptable level. Group-specific thresholds transparent auditable. Unlike -processing fairness methods modify model training, threshold adjustment leaves model untouched — property matters regulated environments model modifications may trigger re-validation requirements.
code: mit <- threshold_optimize(fd, objective = "equalized_odds", min_accuracy = 0.60) mit

title: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
text: Several deliberate choices shape package. First, clinicalfair strictly post-hoc: evaluates models, training procedures. reflects reality fairness audits often conducted teams receive trained model must assess , teams built . Second, package model-agnostic — operates predicted probabilities, model internals. risk score logistic regression, random forest, neural network handled identically. Third, metrics thresholds returned structured data (tibbles), buried console output, enabling programmatic integration reporting pipelines.

title: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
text: Threshold-based mitigation blunt instrument. addresses disparities classification decisions correct model whose learned representations fundamentally biased. Pre-processing (rebalancing training data) -processing (constrained optimisation training) approaches may necessary models deep structural unfairness. clinicalfair implement — scope audit, redesign. four-fifths rule, widely used, universally accepted appropriate threshold clinical applications. fairness scholars argue stricter standards healthcare contexts stakes higher employment decisions. threshold configurable report internals, users consider whether 80% right bar specific clinical domain.

title: Auditing Clinical Prediction Models for Fairness: An R Toolkit Aligned with FDA Expectations
text: included compas_sim dataset provides ready-made example exploring full workflow. Documentation source code available GitHub. clinical AI systems move research prototypes deployed medical devices, fairness auditing transition best practice regulatory requirement. standardised, reproducible toolkit conducting audits — one produces output suitable regulatory review — prerequisite, optional enhancement. Cuiwei Gao health data analyst R developer. clinicalfair available CRAN.
code: install.packages("clinicalfair")

what: Algorithmic Fairness Assessment for Clinical Prediction Models
title: Algorithmic Fairness Assessment for Clinical Prediction Models
text: Algorithmic Fairness Assessment Clinical Prediction Models

title: Algorithmic Fairness Assessment for Clinical Prediction Models
text: clinicalfair post-hoc fairness auditing toolkit clinical prediction models. evaluates existing models computing group-wise fairness metrics, visualizing disparities, performing threshold-based mitigation – motivated regulatory expectations transparency clinical AI. Metrics: demographic parity, equalized odds, predictive parity, AUC, Brier score – optional bootstrap confidence intervals Visualization: disparity plots, ROC group, calibration group Mitigation: group-specific threshold optimization configurable grid resolution (default 0.01) Intersectional: cross-tabulated analysis (race x sex x age) automatic small-group filtering Reporting: four-fifths rule violation detection  Figure 1 | Fairness audit threshold mitigation. () Group-wise selection rate, TPR, FPR equalized-odds threshold optimization. Mitigation reduces cross-group TPR disparity maintaining acceptable accuracy. (b) ROC curves racial group showing differential model performance. Data: COMPAS-style simulated recidivism predictions. Methods: Hardt et al. (2016); Obermeyer et al. (2019).

title: Algorithmic Fairness Assessment for Clinical Prediction Models
text: Existing R packages approach fairness different angles: clinicalfair designed clinical audit use case: clinician regulator receives trained model needs answer “model fair across patient subgroups?” one function call, actionable output.
code: # One-line audit fairness_report(fairness_data(predictions, labels, race))

title: Algorithmic Fairness Assessment for Clinical Prediction Models
code: # From GitHub: devtools::install_github("CuiweiG/clinicalfair")  # After CRAN acceptance: install.packages("clinicalfair")

title: Algorithmic Fairness Assessment for Clinical Prediction Models
text: Figure 2 | Calibration curves racial group. point represents decile bin; point size proportional sample count. Deviation diagonal indicates miscalibration. Differential calibration across groups key fairness concern identified Obermeyer et al. (2019) Science 366:447.
code: library(clinicalfair) data(compas_sim)  # Create fairness evaluation object fd <- fairness_data(     predictions = compas_sim$risk_score,     labels = compas_sim$recidivism,     protected_attr = compas_sim$race )  # Compute metrics fairness_metrics(fd)  # With bootstrap confidence intervals fairness_metrics(fd, ci = TRUE, n_boot = 2000)  # Generate audit report fairness_report(fd)  # Mitigate via threshold optimization threshold_optimize(fd, objective = "equalized_odds")

what: Create a fairness evaluation data object — fairness_data
title: Create a fairness evaluation data object — fairness_data
text: Bundles predictions, labels, protected attributes standardized container fairness analysis.

title: Create a fairness evaluation data object — fairness_data
code: fairness_data(   predictions,   labels,   protected_attr,   threshold = 0.5,   reference_group = NULL )

title: Create a fairness evaluation data object — fairness_data
text: predictions Numeric vector predicted probabilities risk scores (0 1). labels Binary integer vector true outcomes (0 1). protected_attr Character factor vector identifying protected group membership (e.g., race, sex, age group). threshold Decision threshold converting probabilities binary predictions. Default 0.5. reference_group Name reference (privileged) group. NULL, group highest selection rate used.

title: Create a fairness evaluation data object — fairness_data
text: fairness_data object (list) standardized components: predictions, labels, protected, threshold, predicted_class, reference_group, groups, n, prevalence.

title: Create a fairness evaluation data object — fairness_data
code: set.seed(42) fd <- fairness_data(   predictions = runif(200),   labels = rbinom(200, 1, 0.3),   protected_attr = sample(c("GroupA", "GroupB"), 200, replace = TRUE) ) fd #>  #> ── Fairness evaluation data  #> n = 200 | prevalence = 0.265 #> Groups: "GroupA, GroupB" #> Reference: "GroupB" | Threshold: 0.5

what: Compute fairness metrics across groups — fairness_metrics
title: Compute fairness metrics across groups — fairness_metrics
text: Calculates comprehensive set group-wise comparative fairness metrics fairness_data object, optional bootstrap confidence intervals.

title: Compute fairness metrics across groups — fairness_metrics
code: fairness_metrics(   data,   metrics = c("selection_rate", "tpr", "fpr", "ppv", "accuracy", "auc", "brier"),   ci = FALSE,   n_boot = 2000L,   ci_level = 0.95 )

title: Compute fairness metrics across groups — fairness_metrics
text: data fairness_data object. metrics Character vector metrics compute. Default computes available metrics. Options: "selection_rate", "tpr", "fpr", "ppv", "accuracy", "auc", "brier". ci Logical; TRUE, compute bootstrap confidence intervals metric. Default FALSE. n_boot Number bootstrap replicates ci = TRUE. Default 2000. ci_level Confidence level interval. Default 0.95.

title: Compute fairness metrics across groups — fairness_metrics
text: fairness_metrics object (tibble) columns: group, metric, value, ratio (vs reference group), difference (vs reference group). ci = TRUE, additional columns ci_lower ci_upper included.

title: Compute fairness metrics across groups — fairness_metrics
text: Fairness assessed comparing metric values across groups. ratio 1.0 difference 0.0 indicates perfect parity. Common thresholds: ratio \([0.8, 1.25]\) (four-fifths rule, EEOC guidelines) difference < 0.05. ci = TRUE, percentile bootstrap confidence intervals computed resampling within group. accounts sampling variability recommended reporting fairness metrics regulatory publication purposes.

title: Compute fairness metrics across groups — fairness_metrics
text: Obermeyer Z, et al. (2019). Dissecting racial bias algorithm used manage health populations. Science, 366(6464):447–453. doi:10.1126/science.aax2342

title: Compute fairness metrics across groups — fairness_metrics
code: set.seed(42) fd <- fairness_data(   predictions = c(runif(100, 0.2, 0.8), runif(100, 0.3, 0.9)),   labels = c(rbinom(100, 1, 0.3), rbinom(100, 1, 0.5)),   protected_attr = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 100) ) fairness_metrics(fd) #>  #> ── Fairness metrics (reference: B)  #> # A tibble: 14 × 5 #>    group metric         value ratio difference #>  * <chr> <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 A     selection_rate 0.55  0.797  -0.140    #>  2 A     tpr            0.609 0.903  -0.0652   #>  3 A     fpr            0.532 0.757  -0.171    #>  4 A     ppv            0.255 0.567  -0.195    #>  5 A     accuracy       0.5   1.06    0.0300   #>  6 A     brier          0.294 1.00    0.000532 #>  7 A     auc            0.479 0.904  -0.0508   #>  8 B     selection_rate 0.69  1       0        #>  9 B     tpr            0.674 1       0        #> 10 B     fpr            0.704 1       0        #> 11 B     ppv            0.449 1       0        #> 12 B     accuracy       0.47  1       0        #> 13 B     brier          0.294 1       0        #> 14 B     auc            0.530 1       0         # With bootstrap CIs fairness_metrics(fd, ci = TRUE, n_boot = 500) #>  #> ── Fairness metrics (reference: B)  #> # A tibble: 14 × 7 #>    group metric         value ci_lower ci_upper ratio difference #>  * <chr> <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 A     selection_rate 0.55     0.45     0.64  0.797  -0.140    #>  2 A     tpr            0.609    0.366    0.8   0.903  -0.0652   #>  3 A     fpr            0.532    0.424    0.638 0.757  -0.171    #>  4 A     ppv            0.255    0.130    0.371 0.567  -0.195    #>  5 A     accuracy       0.5      0.4      0.6   1.06    0.0300   #>  6 A     brier          0.294    0.259    0.331 1.00    0.000532 #>  7 A     auc            0.479    0.332    0.604 0.904  -0.0508   #>  8 B     selection_rate 0.69     0.59     0.78  1       0        #>  9 B     tpr            0.674    0.542    0.809 1       0        #> 10 B     fpr            0.704    0.577    0.816 1       0        #> 11 B     ppv            0.449    0.343    0.567 1       0        #> 12 B     accuracy       0.47     0.375    0.57  1       0        #> 13 B     brier          0.294    0.254    0.331 1       0        #> 14 B     auc            0.530    0.412    0.642 1       0

what: Generate a fairness summary report — fairness_report
title: Generate a fairness summary report — fairness_report
text: Generate fairness summary report

title: Generate a fairness summary report — fairness_report
code: fairness_report(data, metrics = NULL)

title: Generate a fairness summary report — fairness_report
text: data fairness_data object. metrics fairness_metrics object. NULL, computed automatically.

title: Generate a fairness summary report — fairness_report
text: fairness_report (list) $summary, $flags, $recommendation.

title: Generate a fairness summary report — fairness_report
code: set.seed(42) fd <- fairness_data(   predictions = c(runif(100, 0.2, 0.8), runif(100, 0.3, 0.9)),   labels = c(rbinom(100, 1, 0.3), rbinom(100, 1, 0.5)),   protected_attr = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 100) ) fairness_report(fd) #>  #> ── Fairness Report  #> Reference group: "B" #>  #> ! 3 disparity flag(s): #> A / selection_rate: ratio = 0.797 #> A / fpr: ratio = 0.757 #> A / ppv: ratio = 0.567 #>  #> 3 metric(s) violate the four-fifths rule (ratio outside [0.8, 1.25]). Consider #> threshold adjustment or model recalibration.

title: Compute intersectional fairness metrics — intersectional_fairness
text: Evaluates fairness across combinations multiple protected attributes (e.g., race x sex), revealing disparities hidden single-attribute analysis.

title: Compute intersectional fairness metrics — intersectional_fairness
code: intersectional_fairness(   predictions,   labels,   ...,   threshold = 0.5,   min_group_size = 10L )

title: Compute intersectional fairness metrics — intersectional_fairness
text: predictions Numeric vector predicted probabilities. labels Binary integer vector true outcomes. ... Two named vectors protected attributes. Names become attribute labels. threshold Decision threshold. Default 0.5. min_group_size Minimum number observations required per intersectional group. Groups threshold dropped warning. Default 10.

title: Compute intersectional fairness metrics — intersectional_fairness
text: fairness_metrics object intersectional groups. Groups fewer min_group_size observations excluded.

title: Compute intersectional fairness metrics — intersectional_fairness
text: Buolamwini J, Gebru T (2018). Gender Shades: Intersectional Accuracy Disparities Commercial Gender Classification. Conference Fairness, Accountability Transparency.

title: Compute intersectional fairness metrics — intersectional_fairness
code: set.seed(42) n <- 400 intersectional_fairness(   predictions = runif(n),   labels = rbinom(n, 1, 0.3),   race = sample(c("White", "Black"), n, replace = TRUE),   sex = sample(c("Male", "Female"), n, replace = TRUE) ) #>  #> ── Fairness metrics (reference: White x Male)  #> # A tibble: 28 × 5 #>    group          metric         value ratio difference #>  * <chr>          <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 Black x Female selection_rate 0.463 0.896   -0.0537  #>  2 Black x Female tpr            0.391 1.04     0.0163  #>  3 Black x Female fpr            0.486 0.854   -0.0831  #>  4 Black x Female ppv            0.205 1.05     0.00889 #>  5 Black x Female accuracy       0.484 1.16     0.0685  #>  6 Black x Female brier          0.350 1.03     0.0115  #>  7 Black x Female auc            0.418 0.887   -0.0533  #>  8 Black x Male   selection_rate 0.422 0.817   -0.0948  #>  9 Black x Male   tpr            0.419 1.12     0.0444  #> 10 Black x Male   fpr            0.423 0.743   -0.146   #> # ℹ 18 more rows

title: Optimize thresholds for fairness — threshold_optimize
text: Finds group-specific decision thresholds maximize accuracy subject fairness constraint, minimize disparity subject minimum accuracy constraint.

title: Optimize thresholds for fairness — threshold_optimize
code: threshold_optimize(   data,   objective = c("equalized_odds", "demographic_parity"),   min_accuracy = 0.5,   grid_resolution = 0.01 )

title: Optimize thresholds for fairness — threshold_optimize
text: data fairness_data object. objective "equalized_odds" (default): minimize TPR/FPR disparity across groups. "demographic_parity": equalize selection rates. min_accuracy Minimum acceptable overall accuracy. Default 0.5. grid_resolution Step size threshold grid search. Default 0.01 (99 candidate thresholds). Smaller values give finer-grained optimization modest computational cost.

title: Optimize thresholds for fairness — threshold_optimize
text: fairness_mitigation object (list) : $thresholds (named numeric, one per group), $$(fairness_metrics objects), $accuracy_before $accuracy_after.

title: Optimize thresholds for fairness — threshold_optimize
text: implements post-processing threshold adjustment, simplest transparent mitigation strategy. group receives threshold equalize chosen fairness criterion. "equalized_odds", algorithm computes pooled target TPR FPR across groups original threshold, optimizes every group (including reference) match pooled target. avoids asymmetry fixing reference group threshold adjusting others. clinical applications, group-specific thresholds interpretable auditable, unlike -processing methods modify model .

title: Optimize thresholds for fairness — threshold_optimize
text: Hardt M, Price E, Srebro N (2016). Equality Opportunity Supervised Learning. NeurIPS.

title: Optimize thresholds for fairness — threshold_optimize
code: data(compas_sim) fd <- fairness_data(compas_sim$risk_score, compas_sim$recidivism,                     compas_sim$race) mit <- threshold_optimize(fd) mit #>  #> ── Threshold optimization (equalized_odds)  #> Black: threshold = 0.58 #> White: threshold = 0.42 #>  #> Accuracy: 0.794 -> 0.808

title: Twitter/X Announcement Thread — clinicalfair
text: Tweet 1: FDA expects fairness documentation clinical AI. teams lack standardised toolkit conducting audits. clinicalfair now CRAN — post-hoc fairness assessment clinical prediction models, model-agnostic, regulatory-aligned reporting. #rstats #FDACompliance Tweet 2: : takes predicted probabilities + true labels + protected attributes → computes group-wise TPR, FPR, PPV, AUC, calibration bootstrap CIs → flags four-fifths rule violations → generates audit reports. Works model. #HealthEquity #OpenSource Tweet 3: Intersectional analysis matters. model can look fair race fair sex, yet fail specific subgroups intersection. intersectional_fairness() evaluates combinations multiple protected attributes configurable minimum group sizes. Tweet 4: disparities found, threshold_optimize() searches group-specific decision thresholds equalise error rates maintaining accuracy floor. model stays untouched — critical regulated settings modifications trigger re-validation. #ClinicalAI Tweet 5: Calibration plots group, ROC curves group, disparity bar charts — publication-ready. Includes COMPAS-style simulation dataset immediate exploration. install.packages(“clinicalfair”) https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=clinicalfair — @CuiweiG23 #AlgorithmicFairness #rstats

title: clinicalfair 0.1.1
text: Initial release. fairness_data(): Bundle predictions, labels, protected attributes. fairness_metrics(): Group-wise fairness metrics (selection rate, TPR, FPR, PPV, accuracy, AUC, Brier) optional bootstrap CIs. fairness_report(): Audit report four-fifths rule screening. threshold_optimize(): Group-specific threshold mitigation (equalized odds / demographic parity) configurable grid resolution. intersectional_fairness(): Cross-tabulated multi-attribute analysis small-group filtering. autoplot(), plot_roc(), plot_calibration(): Visualization. Built-simulated COMPAS dataset demonstrations.
